///
/// \class ThreadPool
/// \brief High-performance partitioned thread pool with per-thread queues.
/// Each worker owns a lock-free queue; enqueues go to a random partition.
/// This design minimizes global contention and scales nearly linearly
/// with core count for both enqueue and execution.
///
class ThreadPool {
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal Types
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    struct Task {
        var _fn: (fn ())?;
        var _group: u8 = 0;
    }

    __inline_cpp("using threading = libcxx::thread");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t CACHE_LINE = 64");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE = 1 << 14");

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal queue (bounded lock-free ring buffer)
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    class PartitionQueue {
        priv var _queue: vec::<Task>;
        priv var _head: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _tail: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _cap: usize;

        fn PartitionQueue(self, cap: usize = DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE) {
            self._cap = cap;
            self._queue.resize(cap);
        }

        // delete copy
        delete fn PartitionQueue(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        delete fn op=(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        
        // allow move
        fn op=(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }
        fn PartitionQueue(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }

        fn push(self, task: Task) -> bool {
            var t = self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            var n = (t + 1) % self._cap;
            if n == self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // full
            }
            self._queue[t] = task;
            self._tail.store(n, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn pop(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            var h = self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            if h == self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // empty
            }
            out = self._queue[h];
            self._head.store((h + 1) % self._cap, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn steal(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            return self.pop(out);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // State
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    priv var _threads: vec::<threading>;
    priv var _queues: vec::<PartitionQueue>;
    priv var _groups: array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256> = array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256>();
    priv var _active: libcxx::atomic::<bool> = libcxx::atomic::<bool>(true);
    priv var _count: usize = 0;

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Construction
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn ThreadPool(self, n: usize = threading::hardware_concurrency()) {
        __inline_cpp("
        static_assert(
            libcxx::is_move_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
                && !libcxx::is_copy_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
            , \"PartitionQueue must be move-only\"
        )
        ");
        
        if n == 0 { n = 1; }

        self._count = n;
        self._queues.reserve(n);
        self._threads.reserve(n);

        for _ in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._queues.emplace_back(PartitionQueue());
        }

        for i in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._threads.push_back(threading(fn() { self.worker_loop(i); }));
        }
    }

    fn op delete (self) { self.shutdown(); }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Thread pinning for NUMA/cache locality
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    static fn pin_to_core(core_id: usize) {
        const eval if defined(_WIN32) {
            SetThreadAffinityMask(GetCurrentThread(), (1 << core_id));
        } else if defined(__linux__) {
            var cpuset: cpu_set_t;
            CPU_ZERO(&cpuset);
            CPU_SET(core_id, &cpuset);
            pthread_setaffinity_np(pthread_self(), sizeof(cpu_set_t), &cpuset);
        } else if defined(__APPLE__) {
            var mach_thread: thread_port_t = pthread_mach_thread_np(pthread_self());
            var policy = thread_affinity_policy_data_t {
                affinity_tag: static_cast::<integer_t>(core_id)
            };
            thread_policy_set(mach_thread, THREAD_AFFINITY_POLICY,
                              __inline_cpp("(thread_policy_t)&policy"), 1);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Public Interface
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    pub fn enqueue(self, _fn: fn(), group: u8 = 0) {
        if !self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            return;
        }

        var task = Task();
        task._fn = _fn;
        task._group = group;

        // pick a random queue to avoid contention
        var qid = __inline_cpp("libcxx::random_device()()") % self._count;

        var pushed = self._queues[qid].push(task);
        if !pushed {
            // queue full, fall back to linear scan
            for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                if self._queues[i].push(task) { pushed = true; break; }
            }
        }

        if pushed {
            self._groups[group].fetch_add(1, libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
        } else {
            // catastrophic case (all queues full) - yield to drain
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            self.enqueue(_fn, group);
        }
    }

    pub fn wait_group(self, group: u8) {
        while self._groups[group].load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) > 0 {
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
        }
    }

    pub fn shutdown(self) {
        var expected = true;
        if !self._active.compare_exchange_strong(expected, false) { return; }

        for t: ref!(threading) in self._threads {
            if t.joinable() { t.join(); }
        }
        self._threads.clear();
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Worker Loop
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn worker_loop(self, id: usize) {
        ThreadPool::pin_to_core(id);

        var task: Task;
        while self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            // try own queue first
            var found = self._queues[id].pop(task);

            // if empty, steal from others
            if !found {
                for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                    if (i != id) && (self._queues[i].steal(task)) {
                        found = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            if found && (task._fn != null) {
                try {
                    __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                } catch {
                    std::print("ThreadPool: task threw exception\n");
                }
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            } else {
                __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            }
        }

        // drain leftovers on shutdown
        while self._queues[id].pop(task) {
            if task._fn != null {
                __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            }
        }
    }
}

fn Test() -> i32 {
    std::print("=== Helix ThreadPool Benchmark ===\n");

    var total_timer = Timer("Total test duration");
    var pool = ThreadPool(8);
    var total_tasks: i32 = 4000;
    var groups: i32 = 4;

    std::print(f"Spawning {total_tasks} tasks across {groups} groups...\n");

    __inline_cpp("{");
        var enqueue_timer = Timer("Enqueue phase");
        for var i: i32 = 0; i < total_tasks; ++i {
            var g: u8 = static_cast::<u8>(i % groups);
            pool.enqueue(fn() {
                var acc: f32 = 1.0;
                for var j: i32 = 0; j < 10000000; ++j {
                    acc = acc * 1.000001 + 0.000001;
                }
                if acc == 0 { std::print(""); }
            }, g);
        }
    __inline_cpp("}");

    for var g: i32 = 0; g < groups; ++g {
        var t = Timer(f"Group {g} completion");
        pool.wait_group(static_cast::<u8>(g));
        std::print(f"Group {g} complete.\n");
    }

    var sync_timer = Timer("Final synchronization");
    pool.shutdown();

    std::print("All groups complete.\n");
    return 0;
}

import Core::Timer::*;

__inline_cpp("
#if defined(_WIN32)
#    include <windows.h>
#elif defined(__linux__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <sched.h>
#elif defined(__APPLE__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <mach/thread_act.h>
#    include <mach/mach_init.h>
#endif
");

///
/// \class ThreadPool
/// \brief High-performance partitioned thread pool with per-thread queues.
/// Each worker owns a lock-free queue; enqueues go to a random partition.
/// This design minimizes global contention and scales nearly linearly
/// with core count for both enqueue and execution.
///
class ThreadPool {
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal Types
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    struct Task {
        var _fn: (fn ())?;
        var _group: u8 = 0;
    }

    __inline_cpp("using threading = libcxx::thread");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t CACHE_LINE = 64");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE = 1 << 14");

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal queue (bounded lock-free ring buffer)
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    class PartitionQueue {
        priv var _queue: vec::<Task>;
        priv var _head: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _tail: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _cap: usize;

        fn PartitionQueue(self, cap: usize = DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE) {
            self._cap = cap;
            self._queue.resize(cap);
        }

        // delete copy
        delete fn PartitionQueue(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        delete fn op=(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        
        // allow move
        fn op=(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }
        fn PartitionQueue(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }

        fn push(self, task: Task) -> bool {
            var t = self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            var n = (t + 1) % self._cap;
            if n == self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // full
            }
            self._queue[t] = task;
            self._tail.store(n, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn pop(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            var h = self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            if h == self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // empty
            }
            out = self._queue[h];
            self._head.store((h + 1) % self._cap, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn steal(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            return self.pop(out);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // State
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    priv var _threads: vec::<threading>;
    priv var _queues: vec::<PartitionQueue>;
    priv var _groups: array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256> = array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256>();
    priv var _active: libcxx::atomic::<bool> = libcxx::atomic::<bool>(true);
    priv var _count: usize = 0;

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Construction
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn ThreadPool(self, n: usize = threading::hardware_concurrency()) {
        __inline_cpp("
        static_assert(
            libcxx::is_move_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
                && !libcxx::is_copy_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
            , \"PartitionQueue must be move-only\"
        )
        ");
        
        if n == 0 { n = 1; }

        self._count = n;
        self._queues.reserve(n);
        self._threads.reserve(n);

        for _ in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._queues.emplace_back(PartitionQueue());
        }

        for i in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._threads.push_back(threading(fn() { self.worker_loop(i); }));
        }
    }

    fn op delete (self) { self.shutdown(); }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Thread pinning for NUMA/cache locality
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    static fn pin_to_core(core_id: usize) {
        const eval if defined(_WIN32) {
            SetThreadAffinityMask(GetCurrentThread(), (1 << core_id));
        } else if defined(__linux__) {
            var cpuset: cpu_set_t;
            CPU_ZERO(&cpuset);
            CPU_SET(core_id, &cpuset);
            pthread_setaffinity_np(pthread_self(), sizeof(cpu_set_t), &cpuset);
        } else if defined(__APPLE__) {
            var mach_thread: thread_port_t = pthread_mach_thread_np(pthread_self());
            var policy = thread_affinity_policy_data_t {
                affinity_tag: static_cast::<integer_t>(core_id)
            };
            thread_policy_set(mach_thread, THREAD_AFFINITY_POLICY,
                              __inline_cpp("(thread_policy_t)&policy"), 1);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Public Interface
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    pub fn enqueue(self, _fn: fn(), group: u8 = 0) {
        if !self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            return;
        }

        var task = Task();
        task._fn = _fn;
        task._group = group;

        // pick a random queue to avoid contention
        var qid = __inline_cpp("libcxx::random_device()()") % self._count;

        var pushed = self._queues[qid].push(task);
        if !pushed {
            // queue full, fall back to linear scan
            for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                if self._queues[i].push(task) { pushed = true; break; }
            }
        }

        if pushed {
            self._groups[group].fetch_add(1, libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
        } else {
            // catastrophic case (all queues full) - yield to drain
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            self.enqueue(_fn, group);
        }
    }

    pub fn wait_group(self, group: u8) {
        while self._groups[group].load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) > 0 {
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
        }
    }

    pub fn shutdown(self) {
        var expected = true;
        if !self._active.compare_exchange_strong(expected, false) { return; }

        for t: ref!(threading) in self._threads {
            if t.joinable() { t.join(); }
        }
        self._threads.clear();
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Worker Loop
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn worker_loop(self, id: usize) {
        ThreadPool::pin_to_core(id);

        var task: Task;
        while self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            // try own queue first
            var found = self._queues[id].pop(task);

            // if empty, steal from others
            if !found {
                for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                    if (i != id) && (self._queues[i].steal(task)) {
                        found = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            if found && (task._fn != null) {
                try {
                    __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                } catch {
                    std::print("ThreadPool: task threw exception\n");
                }
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            } else {
                __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            }
        }

        // drain leftovers on shutdown
        while self._queues[id].pop(task) {
            if task._fn != null {
                __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            }
        }
    }
}

fn Test() -> i32 {
    std::print("=== Helix ThreadPool Benchmark ===\n");

    var total_timer = Timer("Total test duration");
    var pool = ThreadPool(8);
    var total_tasks: i32 = 4000;
    var groups: i32 = 4;

    std::print(f"Spawning {total_tasks} tasks across {groups} groups...\n");

    __inline_cpp("{");
        var enqueue_timer = Timer("Enqueue phase");
        for var i: i32 = 0; i < total_tasks; ++i {
            var g: u8 = static_cast::<u8>(i % groups);
            pool.enqueue(fn() {
                var acc: f32 = 1.0;
                for var j: i32 = 0; j < 10000000; ++j {
                    acc = acc * 1.000001 + 0.000001;
                }
                if acc == 0 { std::print(""); }
            }, g);
        }
    __inline_cpp("}");

    for var g: i32 = 0; g < groups; ++g {
        var t = Timer(f"Group {g} completion");
        pool.wait_group(static_cast::<u8>(g));
        std::print(f"Group {g} complete.\n");
    }

    var sync_timer = Timer("Final synchronization");
    pool.shutdown();

    std::print("All groups complete.\n");
    return 0;
}

import Core::Timer::*;

__inline_cpp("
#if defined(_WIN32)
#    include <windows.h>
#elif defined(__linux__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <sched.h>
#elif defined(__APPLE__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <mach/thread_act.h>
#    include <mach/mach_init.h>
#endif
");

///
/// \class ThreadPool
/// \brief High-performance partitioned thread pool with per-thread queues.
/// Each worker owns a lock-free queue; enqueues go to a random partition.
/// This design minimizes global contention and scales nearly linearly
/// with core count for both enqueue and execution.
///
class ThreadPool {
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal Types
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    struct Task {
        var _fn: (fn ())?;
        var _group: u8 = 0;
    }

    __inline_cpp("using threading = libcxx::thread");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t CACHE_LINE = 64");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE = 1 << 14");

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal queue (bounded lock-free ring buffer)
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    class PartitionQueue {
        priv var _queue: vec::<Task>;
        priv var _head: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _tail: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _cap: usize;

        fn PartitionQueue(self, cap: usize = DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE) {
            self._cap = cap;
            self._queue.resize(cap);
        }

        // delete copy
        delete fn PartitionQueue(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        delete fn op=(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        
        // allow move
        fn op=(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }
        fn PartitionQueue(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }

        fn push(self, task: Task) -> bool {
            var t = self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            var n = (t + 1) % self._cap;
            if n == self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // full
            }
            self._queue[t] = task;
            self._tail.store(n, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn pop(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            var h = self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            if h == self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // empty
            }
            out = self._queue[h];
            self._head.store((h + 1) % self._cap, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn steal(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            return self.pop(out);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // State
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    priv var _threads: vec::<threading>;
    priv var _queues: vec::<PartitionQueue>;
    priv var _groups: array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256> = array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256>();
    priv var _active: libcxx::atomic::<bool> = libcxx::atomic::<bool>(true);
    priv var _count: usize = 0;

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Construction
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn ThreadPool(self, n: usize = threading::hardware_concurrency()) {
        __inline_cpp("
        static_assert(
            libcxx::is_move_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
                && !libcxx::is_copy_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
            , \"PartitionQueue must be move-only\"
        )
        ");
        
        if n == 0 { n = 1; }

        self._count = n;
        self._queues.reserve(n);
        self._threads.reserve(n);

        for _ in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._queues.emplace_back(PartitionQueue());
        }

        for i in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._threads.push_back(threading(fn() { self.worker_loop(i); }));
        }
    }

    fn op delete (self) { self.shutdown(); }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Thread pinning for NUMA/cache locality
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    static fn pin_to_core(core_id: usize) {
        const eval if defined(_WIN32) {
            SetThreadAffinityMask(GetCurrentThread(), (1 << core_id));
        } else if defined(__linux__) {
            var cpuset: cpu_set_t;
            CPU_ZERO(&cpuset);
            CPU_SET(core_id, &cpuset);
            pthread_setaffinity_np(pthread_self(), sizeof(cpu_set_t), &cpuset);
        } else if defined(__APPLE__) {
            var mach_thread: thread_port_t = pthread_mach_thread_np(pthread_self());
            var policy = thread_affinity_policy_data_t {
                affinity_tag: static_cast::<integer_t>(core_id)
            };
            thread_policy_set(mach_thread, THREAD_AFFINITY_POLICY,
                              __inline_cpp("(thread_policy_t)&policy"), 1);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Public Interface
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    pub fn enqueue(self, _fn: fn(), group: u8 = 0) {
        if !self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            return;
        }

        var task = Task();
        task._fn = _fn;
        task._group = group;

        // pick a random queue to avoid contention
        var qid = __inline_cpp("libcxx::random_device()()") % self._count;

        var pushed = self._queues[qid].push(task);
        if !pushed {
            // queue full, fall back to linear scan
            for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                if self._queues[i].push(task) { pushed = true; break; }
            }
        }

        if pushed {
            self._groups[group].fetch_add(1, libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
        } else {
            // catastrophic case (all queues full) - yield to drain
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            self.enqueue(_fn, group);
        }
    }

    pub fn wait_group(self, group: u8) {
        while self._groups[group].load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) > 0 {
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
        }
    }

    pub fn shutdown(self) {
        var expected = true;
        if !self._active.compare_exchange_strong(expected, false) { return; }

        for t: ref!(threading) in self._threads {
            if t.joinable() { t.join(); }
        }
        self._threads.clear();
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Worker Loop
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn worker_loop(self, id: usize) {
        ThreadPool::pin_to_core(id);

        var task: Task;
        while self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            // try own queue first
            var found = self._queues[id].pop(task);

            // if empty, steal from others
            if !found {
                for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                    if (i != id) && (self._queues[i].steal(task)) {
                        found = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            if found && (task._fn != null) {
                try {
                    __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                } catch {
                    std::print("ThreadPool: task threw exception\n");
                }
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            } else {
                __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            }
        }

        // drain leftovers on shutdown
        while self._queues[id].pop(task) {
            if task._fn != null {
                __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            }
        }
    }
}

fn Test() -> i32 {
    std::print("=== Helix ThreadPool Benchmark ===\n");

    var total_timer = Timer("Total test duration");
    var pool = ThreadPool(8);
    var total_tasks: i32 = 4000;
    var groups: i32 = 4;

    std::print(f"Spawning {total_tasks} tasks across {groups} groups...\n");

    __inline_cpp("{");
        var enqueue_timer = Timer("Enqueue phase");
        for var i: i32 = 0; i < total_tasks; ++i {
            var g: u8 = static_cast::<u8>(i % groups);
            pool.enqueue(fn() {
                var acc: f32 = 1.0;
                for var j: i32 = 0; j < 10000000; ++j {
                    acc = acc * 1.000001 + 0.000001;
                }
                if acc == 0 { std::print(""); }
            }, g);
        }
    __inline_cpp("}");

    for var g: i32 = 0; g < groups; ++g {
        var t = Timer(f"Group {g} completion");
        pool.wait_group(static_cast::<u8>(g));
        std::print(f"Group {g} complete.\n");
    }

    var sync_timer = Timer("Final synchronization");
    pool.shutdown();

    std::print("All groups complete.\n");
    return 0;
}

import Core::Timer::*;

__inline_cpp("
#if defined(_WIN32)
#    include <windows.h>
#elif defined(__linux__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <sched.h>
#elif defined(__APPLE__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <mach/thread_act.h>
#    include <mach/mach_init.h>
#endif
");

///
/// \class ThreadPool
/// \brief High-performance partitioned thread pool with per-thread queues.
/// Each worker owns a lock-free queue; enqueues go to a random partition.
/// This design minimizes global contention and scales nearly linearly
/// with core count for both enqueue and execution.
///
class ThreadPool {
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal Types
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    struct Task {
        var _fn: (fn ())?;
        var _group: u8 = 0;
    }

    __inline_cpp("using threading = libcxx::thread");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t CACHE_LINE = 64");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE = 1 << 14");

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal queue (bounded lock-free ring buffer)
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    class PartitionQueue {
        priv var _queue: vec::<Task>;
        priv var _head: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _tail: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _cap: usize;

        fn PartitionQueue(self, cap: usize = DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE) {
            self._cap = cap;
            self._queue.resize(cap);
        }

        // delete copy
        delete fn PartitionQueue(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        delete fn op=(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        
        // allow move
        fn op=(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }
        fn PartitionQueue(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }

        fn push(self, task: Task) -> bool {
            var t = self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            var n = (t + 1) % self._cap;
            if n == self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // full
            }
            self._queue[t] = task;
            self._tail.store(n, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn pop(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            var h = self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            if h == self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // empty
            }
            out = self._queue[h];
            self._head.store((h + 1) % self._cap, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn steal(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            return self.pop(out);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // State
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    priv var _threads: vec::<threading>;
    priv var _queues: vec::<PartitionQueue>;
    priv var _groups: array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256> = array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256>();
    priv var _active: libcxx::atomic::<bool> = libcxx::atomic::<bool>(true);
    priv var _count: usize = 0;

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Construction
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn ThreadPool(self, n: usize = threading::hardware_concurrency()) {
        __inline_cpp("
        static_assert(
            libcxx::is_move_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
                && !libcxx::is_copy_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
            , \"PartitionQueue must be move-only\"
        )
        ");
        
        if n == 0 { n = 1; }

        self._count = n;
        self._queues.reserve(n);
        self._threads.reserve(n);

        for _ in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._queues.emplace_back(PartitionQueue());
        }

        for i in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._threads.push_back(threading(fn() { self.worker_loop(i); }));
        }
    }

    fn op delete (self) { self.shutdown(); }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Thread pinning for NUMA/cache locality
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    static fn pin_to_core(core_id: usize) {
        const eval if defined(_WIN32) {
            SetThreadAffinityMask(GetCurrentThread(), (1 << core_id));
        } else if defined(__linux__) {
            var cpuset: cpu_set_t;
            CPU_ZERO(&cpuset);
            CPU_SET(core_id, &cpuset);
            pthread_setaffinity_np(pthread_self(), sizeof(cpu_set_t), &cpuset);
        } else if defined(__APPLE__) {
            var mach_thread: thread_port_t = pthread_mach_thread_np(pthread_self());
            var policy = thread_affinity_policy_data_t {
                affinity_tag: static_cast::<integer_t>(core_id)
            };
            thread_policy_set(mach_thread, THREAD_AFFINITY_POLICY,
                              __inline_cpp("(thread_policy_t)&policy"), 1);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Public Interface
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    pub fn enqueue(self, _fn: fn(), group: u8 = 0) {
        if !self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            return;
        }

        var task = Task();
        task._fn = _fn;
        task._group = group;

        // pick a random queue to avoid contention
        var qid = __inline_cpp("libcxx::random_device()()") % self._count;

        var pushed = self._queues[qid].push(task);
        if !pushed {
            // queue full, fall back to linear scan
            for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                if self._queues[i].push(task) { pushed = true; break; }
            }
        }

        if pushed {
            self._groups[group].fetch_add(1, libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
        } else {
            // catastrophic case (all queues full) - yield to drain
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            self.enqueue(_fn, group);
        }
    }

    pub fn wait_group(self, group: u8) {
        while self._groups[group].load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) > 0 {
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
        }
    }

    pub fn shutdown(self) {
        var expected = true;
        if !self._active.compare_exchange_strong(expected, false) { return; }

        for t: ref!(threading) in self._threads {
            if t.joinable() { t.join(); }
        }
        self._threads.clear();
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Worker Loop
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn worker_loop(self, id: usize) {
        ThreadPool::pin_to_core(id);

        var task: Task;
        while self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            // try own queue first
            var found = self._queues[id].pop(task);

            // if empty, steal from others
            if !found {
                for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                    if (i != id) && (self._queues[i].steal(task)) {
                        found = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            if found && (task._fn != null) {
                try {
                    __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                } catch {
                    std::print("ThreadPool: task threw exception\n");
                }
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            } else {
                __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            }
        }

        // drain leftovers on shutdown
        while self._queues[id].pop(task) {
            if task._fn != null {
                __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            }
        }
    }
}

fn Test() -> i32 {
    std::print("=== Helix ThreadPool Benchmark ===\n");

    var total_timer = Timer("Total test duration");
    var pool = ThreadPool(8);
    var total_tasks: i32 = 4000;
    var groups: i32 = 4;

    std::print(f"Spawning {total_tasks} tasks across {groups} groups...\n");

    __inline_cpp("{");
        var enqueue_timer = Timer("Enqueue phase");
        for var i: i32 = 0; i < total_tasks; ++i {
            var g: u8 = static_cast::<u8>(i % groups);
            pool.enqueue(fn() {
                var acc: f32 = 1.0;
                for var j: i32 = 0; j < 10000000; ++j {
                    acc = acc * 1.000001 + 0.000001;
                }
                if acc == 0 { std::print(""); }
            }, g);
        }
    __inline_cpp("}");

    for var g: i32 = 0; g < groups; ++g {
        var t = Timer(f"Group {g} completion");
        pool.wait_group(static_cast::<u8>(g));
        std::print(f"Group {g} complete.\n");
    }

    var sync_timer = Timer("Final synchronization");
    pool.shutdown();

    std::print("All groups complete.\n");
    return 0;
}

heres the helix code:
```helix
///--- The Helix Project ------------------------------------------------------------------------///
///                                                                                              ///
///   Part of the Helix Project, under the Attribution 4.0 International license (CC BY 4.0).    ///
///   You are allowed to use, modify, redistribute, and create derivative works, even for        ///

///                                                                                              ///
///   SPDX-License-Identifier: CC-BY-4.0                                                         ///
///   Copyright (c) 2024 The Helix Project (CC BY 4.0)                                           ///
///                                                                                              ///
///-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Lib-Helix ---///

import Core::Timer::*;

__inline_cpp("
#if defined(_WIN32)
#    include <windows.h>
#elif defined(__linux__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <sched.h>
#elif defined(__APPLE__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <mach/thread_act.h>
#    include <mach/mach_init.h>
#endif
");

///
/// \class ThreadPool
/// \brief High-performance partitioned thread pool with per-thread queues.
/// Each worker owns a lock-free queue; enqueues go to a random partition.
/// This design minimizes global contention and scales nearly linearly
/// with core count for both enqueue and execution.
///
class ThreadPool {
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal Types
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    struct Task {
        var _fn: (fn ())?;
        var _group: u8 = 0;
    }

    __inline_cpp("using threading = libcxx::thread");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t CACHE_LINE = 64");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE = 1 << 14");

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal queue (bounded lock-free ring buffer)
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    class PartitionQueue {
        priv var _queue: vec::<Task>;
        priv var _head: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _tail: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _cap: usize;

        fn PartitionQueue(self, cap: usize = DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE) {
            self._cap = cap;
            self._queue.resize(cap);
        }

        // delete copy
        delete fn PartitionQueue(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        delete fn op=(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        
        // allow move
        fn op=(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }
        fn PartitionQueue(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }

        fn push(self, task: Task) -> bool {
            var t = self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            var n = (t + 1) % self._cap;
            if n == self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // full
            }
            self._queue[t] = task;
            self._tail.store(n, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn pop(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            var h = self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            if h == self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // empty
            }
            out = self._queue[h];
            self._head.store((h + 1) % self._cap, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn steal(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            return self.pop(out);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // State
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    priv var _threads: vec::<threading>;
    priv var _queues: vec::<PartitionQueue>;
    priv var _groups: array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256> = array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256>();
    priv var _active: libcxx::atomic::<bool> = libcxx::atomic::<bool>(true);
    priv var _count: usize = 0;

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Construction
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn ThreadPool(self, n: usize = threading::hardware_concurrency()) {
        __inline_cpp("

            libcxx::is_move_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
                && !libcxx::is_copy_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
            , \"PartitionQueue must be move-only\"
        )
        ");
        
        if n == 0 { n = 1; }

        self._count = n;
        self._queues.reserve(n);
        self._threads.reserve(n);

        for _ in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._queues.emplace_back(PartitionQueue());
        }

        for i in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._threads.push_back(threading(fn() { self.worker_loop(i); }));
        }
    }

    fn op delete (self) { self.shutdown(); }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Thread pinning for NUMA/cache locality
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    static fn pin_to_core(core_id: usize) {
        const eval if defined(_WIN32) {
            SetThreadAffinityMask(GetCurrentThread(), (1 << core_id));

            CPU_SET(core_id, &cpuset);

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Public Interface

    pub fn enqueue(self, _fn: fn(), group: u8 = 0) {

            return;
        }

        var task = Task();
        task._fn = _fn;
        task._group = group;

        // pick a random queue to avoid contention
        var qid = __inline_cpp("libcxx::random_device()()") % self._count;

        var pushed = self._queues[qid].push(task);
        if !pushed {

        }
    }

    pub fn shutdown(self) {

        if !self._active.compare_exchange_strong(expected, false) { return; }

        for t: ref!(threading) in self._threads {
            if t.joinable() { t.join(); }
        }
        self._threads.clear();
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Worker Loop
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn worker_loop(self, id: usize) {
        ThreadPool::pin_to_core(id);

        var task: Task;
        while self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            // try own queue first
            var found = self._queues[id].pop(task);

            // if empty, steal from others
            if !found {

                    if (i != id) && (self._queues[i].steal(task)) {

            } else {
                __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            }
        }

        // drain leftovers on shutdown
        while self._queues[id].pop(task) {
            if task._fn != null {
                __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            }
        }
    }
}

fn Test() -> i32 {
    std::print("=== Helix ThreadPool Benchmark ===\n");

    var total_timer = Timer("Total test duration");
    var pool = ThreadPool(8);
    var total_tasks: i32 = 4000;
    var groups: i32 = 4;

    std::print(f"Spawning {total_tasks} tasks across {groups} groups...\n");

    __inline_cpp("{");

        for var i: i32 = 0; i < total_tasks; ++i {
            var g: u8 = static_cast::<u8>(i % groups);
            pool.enqueue(fn() {
                var acc: f32 = 1.0;
                for var j: i32 = 0; j < 10000000; ++j {
                    acc = acc * 1.000001 + 0.000001;
                }
                if acc == 0 { std::print(""); }
            }, g);
        }
    __inline_cpp("}");

    for var g: i32 = 0; g < groups; ++g {
        var t = Timer(f"Group {g} completion");
        pool.wait_group(static_cast::<u8>(g));
        std::print(f"Group {g} complete.\n");
    }

    var sync_timer = Timer("Final synchronization");
    pool.shutdown();

    std::print("All groups complete.\n");
    return 0;
}

///
/// \class ThreadPool
/// \brief High-performance partitioned thread pool with per-thread queues.
/// Each worker owns a lock-free queue; enqueues go to a random partition.
/// This design minimizes global contention and scales nearly linearly
/// with core count for both enqueue and execution.
///
class ThreadPool {
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal Types
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    struct Task {
        var _fn: (fn ())?;
        var _group: u8 = 0;
    }

    __inline_cpp("using threading = libcxx::thread");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t CACHE_LINE = 64");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE = 1 << 14");

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal queue (bounded lock-free ring buffer)
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    class PartitionQueue {
        priv var _queue: vec::<Task>;
        priv var _head: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _tail: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _cap: usize;

        fn PartitionQueue(self, cap: usize = DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE) {
            self._cap = cap;
            self._queue.resize(cap);
        }

        // delete copy
        delete fn PartitionQueue(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        delete fn op=(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        
        // allow move
        fn op=(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }
        fn PartitionQueue(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }

        fn push(self, task: Task) -> bool {
            var t = self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            var n = (t + 1) % self._cap;
            if n == self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // full
            }
            self._queue[t] = task;
            self._tail.store(n, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn pop(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            var h = self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            if h == self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // empty
            }
            out = self._queue[h];
            self._head.store((h + 1) % self._cap, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn steal(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            return self.pop(out);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // State
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    priv var _threads: vec::<threading>;
    priv var _queues: vec::<PartitionQueue>;
    priv var _groups: array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256> = array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256>();
    priv var _active: libcxx::atomic::<bool> = libcxx::atomic::<bool>(true);
    priv var _count: usize = 0;

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Construction
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn ThreadPool(self, n: usize = threading::hardware_concurrency()) {
        __inline_cpp("
        static_assert(
            libcxx::is_move_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
                && !libcxx::is_copy_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
            , \"PartitionQueue must be move-only\"
        )
        ");
        
        if n == 0 { n = 1; }

        self._count = n;
        self._queues.reserve(n);
        self._threads.reserve(n);

        for _ in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._queues.emplace_back(PartitionQueue());
        }

        for i in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._threads.push_back(threading(fn() { self.worker_loop(i); }));
        }
    }

    fn op delete (self) { self.shutdown(); }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Thread pinning for NUMA/cache locality
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    static fn pin_to_core(core_id: usize) {
        const eval if defined(_WIN32) {
            SetThreadAffinityMask(GetCurrentThread(), (1 << core_id));
        } else if defined(__linux__) {
            var cpuset: cpu_set_t;
            CPU_ZERO(&cpuset);
            CPU_SET(core_id, &cpuset);
            pthread_setaffinity_np(pthread_self(), sizeof(cpu_set_t), &cpuset);
        } else if defined(__APPLE__) {
            var mach_thread: thread_port_t = pthread_mach_thread_np(pthread_self());
            var policy = thread_affinity_policy_data_t {
                affinity_tag: static_cast::<integer_t>(core_id)
            };
            thread_policy_set(mach_thread, THREAD_AFFINITY_POLICY,
                              __inline_cpp("(thread_policy_t)&policy"), 1);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Public Interface
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    pub fn enqueue(self, _fn: fn(), group: u8 = 0) {
        if !self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            return;
        }

        var task = Task();
        task._fn = _fn;
        task._group = group;

        // pick a random queue to avoid contention
        var qid = __inline_cpp("libcxx::random_device()()") % self._count;

        var pushed = self._queues[qid].push(task);
        if !pushed {
            // queue full, fall back to linear scan
            for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                if self._queues[i].push(task) { pushed = true; break; }
            }
        }

        if pushed {
            self._groups[group].fetch_add(1, libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
        } else {
            // catastrophic case (all queues full) - yield to drain
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            self.enqueue(_fn, group);
        }
    }

    pub fn wait_group(self, group: u8) {
        while self._groups[group].load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) > 0 {
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
        }
    }

    pub fn shutdown(self) {
        var expected = true;
        if !self._active.compare_exchange_strong(expected, false) { return; }

        for t: ref!(threading) in self._threads {
            if t.joinable() { t.join(); }
        }
        self._threads.clear();
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Worker Loop
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn worker_loop(self, id: usize) {
        ThreadPool::pin_to_core(id);

        var task: Task;
        while self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            // try own queue first
            var found = self._queues[id].pop(task);

            // if empty, steal from others
            if !found {
                for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                    if (i != id) && (self._queues[i].steal(task)) {
                        found = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            if found && (task._fn != null) {
                try {
                    __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                } catch {
                    std::print("ThreadPool: task threw exception\n");
                }
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            } else {
                __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            }
        }

        // drain leftovers on shutdown
        while self._queues[id].pop(task) {
            if task._fn != null {
                __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            }
        }
    }
}

fn Test() -> i32 {
    std::print("=== Helix ThreadPool Benchmark ===\n");

    var total_timer = Timer("Total test duration");
    var pool = ThreadPool(8);
    var total_tasks: i32 = 4000;
    var groups: i32 = 4;

    std::print(f"Spawning {total_tasks} tasks across {groups} groups...\n");

    __inline_cpp("{");
        var enqueue_timer = Timer("Enqueue phase");
        for var i: i32 = 0; i < total_tasks; ++i {
            var g: u8 = static_cast::<u8>(i % groups);
            pool.enqueue(fn() {
                var acc: f32 = 1.0;
                for var j: i32 = 0; j < 10000000; ++j {
                    acc = acc * 1.000001 + 0.000001;
                }
                if acc == 0 { std::print(""); }
            }, g);
        }
    __inline_cpp("}");

    for var g: i32 = 0; g < groups; ++g {
        var t = Timer(f"Group {g} completion");
        pool.wait_group(static_cast::<u8>(g));
        std::print(f"Group {g} complete.\n");
    }

    var sync_timer = Timer("Final synchronization");
    pool.shutdown();

    std::print("All groups complete.\n");
    return 0;
}

import Core::Timer::*;

__inline_cpp("
#if defined(_WIN32)
#    include <windows.h>
#elif defined(__linux__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <sched.h>
#elif defined(__APPLE__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <mach/thread_act.h>
#    include <mach/mach_init.h>
#endif
");

///
/// \class ThreadPool
/// \brief High-performance partitioned thread pool with per-thread queues.
/// Each worker owns a lock-free queue; enqueues go to a random partition.
/// This design minimizes global contention and scales nearly linearly
/// with core count for both enqueue and execution.
///
class ThreadPool {
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal Types
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    struct Task {
        var _fn: (fn ())?;
        var _group: u8 = 0;
    }

    __inline_cpp("using threading = libcxx::thread");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t CACHE_LINE = 64");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE = 1 << 14");

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal queue (bounded lock-free ring buffer)
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    class PartitionQueue {
        priv var _queue: vec::<Task>;
        priv var _head: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _tail: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _cap: usize;

        fn PartitionQueue(self, cap: usize = DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE) {
            self._cap = cap;
            self._queue.resize(cap);
        }

        // delete copy
        delete fn PartitionQueue(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        delete fn op=(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        
        // allow move
        fn op=(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }
        fn PartitionQueue(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }

        fn push(self, task: Task) -> bool {
            var t = self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            var n = (t + 1) % self._cap;
            if n == self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // full
            }
            self._queue[t] = task;
            self._tail.store(n, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn pop(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            var h = self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            if h == self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // empty
            }
            out = self._queue[h];
            self._head.store((h + 1) % self._cap, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn steal(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            return self.pop(out);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // State
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    priv var _threads: vec::<threading>;
    priv var _queues: vec::<PartitionQueue>;
    priv var _groups: array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256> = array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256>();
    priv var _active: libcxx::atomic::<bool> = libcxx::atomic::<bool>(true);
    priv var _count: usize = 0;

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Construction
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn ThreadPool(self, n: usize = threading::hardware_concurrency()) {
        __inline_cpp("
        static_assert(
            libcxx::is_move_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
                && !libcxx::is_copy_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
            , \"PartitionQueue must be move-only\"
        )
        ");
        
        if n == 0 { n = 1; }

        self._count = n;
        self._queues.reserve(n);
        self._threads.reserve(n);

        for _ in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._queues.emplace_back(PartitionQueue());
        }

        for i in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._threads.push_back(threading(fn() { self.worker_loop(i); }));
        }
    }

    fn op delete (self) { self.shutdown(); }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Thread pinning for NUMA/cache locality
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    static fn pin_to_core(core_id: usize) {
        const eval if defined(_WIN32) {
            SetThreadAffinityMask(GetCurrentThread(), (1 << core_id));
        } else if defined(__linux__) {
            var cpuset: cpu_set_t;
            CPU_ZERO(&cpuset);
            CPU_SET(core_id, &cpuset);
            pthread_setaffinity_np(pthread_self(), sizeof(cpu_set_t), &cpuset);
        } else if defined(__APPLE__) {
            var mach_thread: thread_port_t = pthread_mach_thread_np(pthread_self());
            var policy = thread_affinity_policy_data_t {
                affinity_tag: static_cast::<integer_t>(core_id)
            };
            thread_policy_set(mach_thread, THREAD_AFFINITY_POLICY,
                              __inline_cpp("(thread_policy_t)&policy"), 1);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Public Interface
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    pub fn enqueue(self, _fn: fn(), group: u8 = 0) {
        if !self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            return;
        }

        var task = Task();
        task._fn = _fn;
        task._group = group;

        // pick a random queue to avoid contention
        var qid = __inline_cpp("libcxx::random_device()()") % self._count;

        var pushed = self._queues[qid].push(task);
        if !pushed {
            // queue full, fall back to linear scan
            for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                if self._queues[i].push(task) { pushed = true; break; }
            }
        }

        if pushed {
            self._groups[group].fetch_add(1, libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
        } else {
            // catastrophic case (all queues full) - yield to drain
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            self.enqueue(_fn, group);
        }
    }

    pub fn wait_group(self, group: u8) {
        while self._groups[group].load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) > 0 {
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
        }
    }

    pub fn shutdown(self) {
        var expected = true;
        if !self._active.compare_exchange_strong(expected, false) { return; }

        for t: ref!(threading) in self._threads {
            if t.joinable() { t.join(); }
        }
        self._threads.clear();
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Worker Loop
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn worker_loop(self, id: usize) {
        ThreadPool::pin_to_core(id);

        var task: Task;
        while self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            // try own queue first
            var found = self._queues[id].pop(task);

            // if empty, steal from others
            if !found {
                for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                    if (i != id) && (self._queues[i].steal(task)) {
                        found = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            if found && (task._fn != null) {
                try {
                    __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                } catch {
                    std::print("ThreadPool: task threw exception\n");
                }
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            } else {
                __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            }
        }

        // drain leftovers on shutdown
        while self._queues[id].pop(task) {
            if task._fn != null {
                __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            }
        }
    }
}

fn Test() -> i32 {
    std::print("=== Helix ThreadPool Benchmark ===\n");

    var total_timer = Timer("Total test duration");
    var pool = ThreadPool(8);
    var total_tasks: i32 = 4000;
    var groups: i32 = 4;

    std::print(f"Spawning {total_tasks} tasks across {groups} groups...\n");

    __inline_cpp("{");
        var enqueue_timer = Timer("Enqueue phase");
        for var i: i32 = 0; i < total_tasks; ++i {
            var g: u8 = static_cast::<u8>(i % groups);
            pool.enqueue(fn() {
                var acc: f32 = 1.0;
                for var j: i32 = 0; j < 10000000; ++j {
                    acc = acc * 1.000001 + 0.000001;
                }
                if acc == 0 { std::print(""); }
            }, g);
        }
    __inline_cpp("}");

    for var g: i32 = 0; g < groups; ++g {
        var t = Timer(f"Group {g} completion");
        pool.wait_group(static_cast::<u8>(g));
        std::print(f"Group {g} complete.\n");
    }

    var sync_timer = Timer("Final synchronization");
    pool.shutdown();

    std::print("All groups complete.\n");
    return 0;
}

import Core::Timer::*;

__inline_cpp("
#if defined(_WIN32)
#    include <windows.h>
#elif defined(__linux__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <sched.h>
#elif defined(__APPLE__)
#    include <pthread.h>
#    include <mach/thread_act.h>
#    include <mach/mach_init.h>
#endif
");

///
/// \class ThreadPool
/// \brief High-performance partitioned thread pool with per-thread queues.
/// Each worker owns a lock-free queue; enqueues go to a random partition.
/// This design minimizes global contention and scales nearly linearly
/// with core count for both enqueue and execution.
///
class ThreadPool {
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal Types
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    struct Task {
        var _fn: (fn ())?;
        var _group: u8 = 0;
    }

    __inline_cpp("using threading = libcxx::thread");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t CACHE_LINE = 64");
    __inline_cpp("static constexpr size_t DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE = 1 << 14");

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Internal queue (bounded lock-free ring buffer)
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    class PartitionQueue {
        priv var _queue: vec::<Task>;
        priv var _head: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _tail: libcxx::atomic::<usize> = libcxx::atomic::<usize>(0);
        priv var _cap: usize;

        fn PartitionQueue(self, cap: usize = DEFAULT_QUEUE_SIZE) {
            self._cap = cap;
            self._queue.resize(cap);
        }

        // delete copy
        delete fn PartitionQueue(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        delete fn op=(self, other: ref!(PartitionQueue));
        
        // allow move
        fn op=(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }
        fn PartitionQueue(self, other: mref!(PartitionQueue)) {
            self._cap   = other._cap;
            self._queue = libcxx::move(other._queue);
            self._head.store(other._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
            self._tail.store(other._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed));
        }

        fn push(self, task: Task) -> bool {
            var t = self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            var n = (t + 1) % self._cap;
            if n == self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // full
            }
            self._queue[t] = task;
            self._tail.store(n, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn pop(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            var h = self._head.load(libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
            if h == self._tail.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
                return false; // empty
            }
            out = self._queue[h];
            self._head.store((h + 1) % self._cap, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            return true;
        }

        fn steal(self, out: ref!(Task)) -> bool {
            return self.pop(out);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // State
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    priv var _threads: vec::<threading>;
    priv var _queues: vec::<PartitionQueue>;
    priv var _groups: array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256> = array::<libcxx::atomic::<u64>, 256>();
    priv var _active: libcxx::atomic::<bool> = libcxx::atomic::<bool>(true);
    priv var _count: usize = 0;

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Construction
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn ThreadPool(self, n: usize = threading::hardware_concurrency()) {
        __inline_cpp("
        static_assert(
            libcxx::is_move_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
                && !libcxx::is_copy_constructible_v<PartitionQueue>
            , \"PartitionQueue must be move-only\"
        )
        ");
        
        if n == 0 { n = 1; }

        self._count = n;
        self._queues.reserve(n);
        self._threads.reserve(n);

        for _ in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._queues.emplace_back(PartitionQueue());
        }

        for i in range::<usize>(0, n) {
            self._threads.push_back(threading(fn() { self.worker_loop(i); }));
        }
    }

    fn op delete (self) { self.shutdown(); }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Thread pinning for NUMA/cache locality
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    static fn pin_to_core(core_id: usize) {
        const eval if defined(_WIN32) {
            SetThreadAffinityMask(GetCurrentThread(), (1 << core_id));
        } else if defined(__linux__) {
            var cpuset: cpu_set_t;
            CPU_ZERO(&cpuset);
            CPU_SET(core_id, &cpuset);
            pthread_setaffinity_np(pthread_self(), sizeof(cpu_set_t), &cpuset);
        } else if defined(__APPLE__) {
            var mach_thread: thread_port_t = pthread_mach_thread_np(pthread_self());
            var policy = thread_affinity_policy_data_t {
                affinity_tag: static_cast::<integer_t>(core_id)
            };
            thread_policy_set(mach_thread, THREAD_AFFINITY_POLICY,
                              __inline_cpp("(thread_policy_t)&policy"), 1);
        }
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Public Interface
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    pub fn enqueue(self, _fn: fn(), group: u8 = 0) {
        if !self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            return;
        }

        var task = Task();
        task._fn = _fn;
        task._group = group;

        // pick a random queue to avoid contention
        var qid = __inline_cpp("libcxx::random_device()()") % self._count;

        var pushed = self._queues[qid].push(task);
        if !pushed {
            // queue full, fall back to linear scan
            for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                if self._queues[i].push(task) { pushed = true; break; }
            }
        }

        if pushed {
            self._groups[group].fetch_add(1, libcxx::memory_order::relaxed);
        } else {
            // catastrophic case (all queues full) - yield to drain
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            self.enqueue(_fn, group);
        }
    }

    pub fn wait_group(self, group: u8) {
        while self._groups[group].load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) > 0 {
            __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
        }
    }

    pub fn shutdown(self) {
        var expected = true;
        if !self._active.compare_exchange_strong(expected, false) { return; }

        for t: ref!(threading) in self._threads {
            if t.joinable() { t.join(); }
        }
        self._threads.clear();
    }

    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    // Worker Loop
    //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    fn worker_loop(self, id: usize) {
        ThreadPool::pin_to_core(id);

        var task: Task;
        while self._active.load(libcxx::memory_order::acquire) {
            // try own queue first
            var found = self._queues[id].pop(task);

            // if empty, steal from others
            if !found {
                for var i: usize = 0; i < self._count; ++i {
                    if (i != id) && (self._queues[i].steal(task)) {
                        found = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            if found && (task._fn != null) {
                try {
                    __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                } catch {
                    std::print("ThreadPool: task threw exception\n");
                }
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            } else {
                __inline_cpp("libcxx::this_thread::yield()");
            }
        }

        // drain leftovers on shutdown
        while self._queues[id].pop(task) {
            if task._fn != null {
                __inline_cpp("(*task._fn)();");
                self._groups[task._group].fetch_sub(1, libcxx::memory_order::release);
            }
        }
    }
}

fn Test() -> i32 {
    std::print("=== Helix ThreadPool Benchmark ===\n");

    var total_timer = Timer("Total test duration");
    var pool = ThreadPool(8);
    var total_tasks: i32 = 4000;
    var groups: i32 = 4;

    std::print(f"Spawning {total_tasks} tasks across {groups} groups...\n");

    __inline_cpp("{");
        var enqueue_timer = Timer("Enqueue phase");
        for var i: i32 = 0; i < total_tasks; ++i {
            var g: u8 = static_cast::<u8>(i % groups);
            pool.enqueue(fn() {
                var acc: f32 = 1.0;
                for var j: i32 = 0; j < 10000000; ++j {
                    acc = acc * 1.000001 + 0.000001;
                }
                if acc == 0 { std::print(""); }
            }, g);
        }
    __inline_cpp("}");

    for var g: i32 = 0; g < groups; ++g {
        var t = Timer(f"Group {g} completion");
        pool.wait_group(static_cast::<u8>(g));
        std::print(f"Group {g} complete.\n");
    }

    var sync_timer = Timer("Final synchronization");
    pool.shutdown();

    std::print("All groups complete.\n");
    return 0;
}
